input_row.title: Ensure 'Allowed Protocols' for shared access signature (SAS) tokens is set to 'HTTPS Only'
input_row.description: Shared access signatures (SAS) can be used to grant limited access to Azure Storage resources. When generating a SAS, it is possible to specify the allowed protocols for a request made with the SAS. It is recommended to allow requests over HTTPS only.
input_row.rationale: If a SAS is passed over HTTP and intercepted, an attacker performing a man-in-the- middle attack can read the SAS. Then, they can use that SAS just as the intended user could have. This can potentially compromise sensitive data or allow for data corruption by the malicious user. Impact: SAS can pose security risks if they are not managed carefully.
input_row.remediation: Default Value: When generating a SAS, the default selection for Allowed protocols is HTTPS only. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-sas-overview 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-account-keys- manage Additional Information: This recommendation forms the basis for the recommendations with the same title in the following sections: • Azure Blob Storage • Queue Storage • Storage Accounts > Secrets and Keys
input_row.impact: SAS can pose security risks if they are not managed carefully.
input_row.reference: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-sas-overview 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/storage-account-keys- manage Additional Information: This recommendation forms the basis for the recommendations with the same title in the following sections: • Azure Blob Storage • Queue Storage • Storage Accounts > Secrets and Keys

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Navigate to the Azure Portal and select "Storage Accounts."
2. Choose the specific storage account that you want to assess.
3. In the storage account blade, go to the "Shared access signature" section (usually found in the Settings group).
4. Verify the configuration settings for the "Allowed protocols" for SAS tokens.
5. Check that the option is set to "HTTPS Only."
6. Confirm that no SAS tokens have been created with "HTTP" or "HTTP and HTTPS" as allowed protocols.

**Expected secure configuration:** 
- The "Allowed protocols" should explicitly be set to "HTTPS Only."

**How to confirm compliance:**
- If "Allowed protocols" is set to the appropriate level (HTTPS Only), document this as compliant. If it is set to allow HTTP, note it as non-compliant and take further action based on your organizational policy for remediation.
